target.title: feat(voice-call): msteams — enterprise & governance features + natural extensions of the CVI work (deterministic meeting etiquette, verbal interrupts, retroactive vision, per-thread sessions)
target.body:
## Summary

A focused increment on top of **#91438** (the Microsoft Teams `msteams` voice/video provider) that takes the Conversational Video Interface from "works" to **enterprise-ready**: the live **self-answer (echo) bug is fixed**, the bridge handshake is **replay-proof**, group-call etiquette and **verbal interrupts are deterministic** (not model-trusted), the agent gains **retroactive vision** ("what did the earlier slide say?"), sessions become **thread-aware**, and visual sharing gets **captions + slideshows** with a **"thinking" face** while the agent works.

> **Stacked on #91438 — review just the last 18 commits** (`5249ad9bbb…2110577317`): **13 files, +1,277/−227** (478 of the additions are tests). The PR's full diff currently includes #91438's commits only because that PR hasn't landed yet; once #91438 merges, this branch rebases to exactly the increment. #91438 stays the canonical foundation PR — this one deliberately does **not** restate or replace it.

### Foundation recap (from #91438, included in this diff until it lands)

The `msteams` provider: inbound Teams voice + video vision (`look_at_screen`, streaming auto-attach, realtime continuous vision via `RealtimeVoiceBridge.sendImage`), group/meeting awareness (per-participant attribution + speak-only-when-addressed), outbound "call me back" with voicemail fallback, avatar rendering drivers (expression cues, viseme lip-shape, `show_to_caller` visual sharing), caller allowlist (closed by default), recording-status gate on every media-derived path, HMAC-authenticated bridge WebSocket, SSRF-guarded outbound. Live-proven on 1:1 and 3-party group Teams calls (see #91438's proof).

## 🛡️ Enterprise / governance

- **Single-use HMAC upgrade tuples (replay guard)** — a captured bridge handshake replayed within the HMAC window could open a ghost session once the live call ended (the duplicate-callId check only protects while connected). Verified `(callId, timestamp, signature)` tuples are now single-use; a legitimate reconnect always carries a fresh timestamp. Only verified tuples are recorded (no unauthenticated map growth); entries expire with the window. No worker change required. Tested: connect OK → exact replay rejected (401) → fresh-timestamp reconnect OK.
- **Deterministic group-call gate on the realtime path** — "speak only when addressed" was instruction-only (model-enforced) on realtime. Every finished caller turn is now evaluated with the same `shouldRespondToGroupTurn` core the streaming path uses, and an unaddressed meeting turn's reply **audio is dropped at the egress** — the etiquette holds even if the model ignores instructions. Participant count wired live; 1:1 calls never gate.
- **Per-thread sessions (context isolation)** — `sessionScope: "per-thread"` keys the agent session `voice:thread:<threadId>` by the Teams meeting/chat thread on both paths, so parallel conversations can't pollute each other's context and a recurring meeting keeps its context across calls. Providers without threads (PSTN) fall through to per-phone unchanged; events carry an additive optional `threadId`.
- The recording-status gate (Media Access API) continues to cover every media-derived path the new features touch.

## 📞 Call experience

- **Fix: realtime self-answer (echo)** — live calls showed the bot answering its own voice: the caller leg carries the bot's audio back as acoustic echo and the realtime server-VAD replied to it. A **half-duplex echo guard** (default ON) drops caller input while the bot's audio is playing out **unless it is loud enough to be a genuine barge-in** (RMS gate). Keyed on a **playout clock** — the model streams audio faster than realtime, so last-send time is not play-time; the estimate accumulates each chunk's PCM duration and collapses on barge-in flush. The streaming/STT path shares the same predicate. Regression test: burst 10 s of audio → echo still suppressed at +2 s. Tunable via `realtime.echoSuppressionWindowMs` / `realtime.echoBargeInRms`.
- **Deterministic verbal interrupts** — "stop", "wait", "hold on", "never mind" are handled in code, not by the model (which is mid-generation when they arrive): streaming cuts playback **and suppresses the reply** (the bot goes quiet instead of answering "stop"); realtime flushes the worker queue immediately even if server-VAD interruption lagged. Whole-utterance matching with filler **and configured wake-phrase stripping** — *"⟨name⟩, stop"* cuts as instantly as a bare *"stop"*, while *"stop by the store"* never triggers.
- **Fix: outbound notify call-backs clipped/lingered** — the realtime path now honors the call mode: **notify** (the outbound default) states the result and auto-hangs-up **after the audio has actually drained** (playout-clock based, long results never clip) + `notifyHangupDelaySec` grace; **conversation** keeps deliver-then-chat.

## 🎥 Natural extensions of the CVI work

- **Retroactive vision (frame history)** — the vision store keeps a ring of **16 scene-change keyframes** per call (the worker only emits changed frames); `look_at_screen` gains `scope: "history"`, attaching up to 6 timestamped, participant-attributed keyframes to **one budgeted consult** — so the agent can answer *"what did the earlier slide say?"* or *"catch me up on what was shown"*. Deliberately no eager OCR-per-keyframe (that would burn a vision call per scene change — the model reads at question time, same `maxVisionPerMinute` philosophy).
- **Scene-change-driven realtime vision** — inbound frames are pushed to the realtime model **on arrival**, so it sees changes promptly instead of up to ~6 s late; the ambient poll remains as a backstop. Same change-dedup + budget.
- **Camera + screen simultaneous awareness** — the ambient push forwards **both** sources with per-source change-dedup and labels, so a caller on camera while sharing a screen is fully seen.
- **`show_to_caller` captions + slideshow** — the agent's one-line summary rides `display.image` as a `caption` (renderer draws a strip along the bottom); multi-image results play as a background-paced slideshow (previously only the last image was visible) that stops if the call ends. Additive protocol — an older renderer ignores both.
- **"Thinking" avatar expression** — while a waiting tool runs (consult / `look_at_screen` / `show_to_caller`), the avatar shows a thinking face; the reply re-cues the real emotion when the result lands (and can't stick if the model stays silent).

## Code quality / refactoring

- **`MsteamsVisionStore`** (per-call frames + history ring + vision budget) and **`msteams-tts-playback.ts`** (TTS synthesis + expression/viseme cues + the paced 20 ms frame streamer) extracted from the ~1.3k-line provider.
- **`shouldSuppressEcho`** — one shared echo predicate for the realtime + streaming paths; **`isVerbalInterrupt`** — one lexical interrupt matcher (unit-tested) for both paths.

## Config / compatibility notes

- `realtime.suppressInputDuringPlayback` **semantics changed**: previously opt-in (default off), now the echo guard is **on by default** — set `false` to disable. (Default-off was the live self-answer bug.)
- Outbound realtime call-backs now follow `outbound.defaultMode` (default **notify** = deliver-and-hang-up). Set `"conversation"` to keep them conversational.
- New optional keys: `realtime.echoSuppressionWindowMs`, `realtime.echoBargeInRms`; new `sessionScope` value `"per-thread"`; new optional event field `threadId` (additive).
- All bridge protocol additions are additive (`display.image.caption`, slideshow `durationMs`); an older worker ignores them. The replay guard requires no worker change.

## Relationship to #91438 & follow-ups

**Depends on #91438** (the msteams provider foundation) — stacked, not superseding. That PR carries the provider/vision/group/outbound/rendering foundation plus its live-call proof; this PR contains only the increment above. The shared history disappears from this diff when #91438 lands.

Planned follow-up tracks (separate PRs, not in this diff): meeting summarization + minutes artifact, live Arabic↔English translation, chat-side features (message actions, proactive notifications, voice notes), and governance (AAD-tier tool policies, audit channel, DLP redaction).

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior addressed:** The realtime self-answer (echo) bug observed on live Teams calls, bridge handshake replay, non-deterministic group gating on realtime, notify call-back clipping, and the verbal-interrupt / retroactive-vision / per-thread-session / visual-sharing features listed above.
- **Real environment tested:** Foundation behavior was live-proven on real 1:1 and 3-party group Microsoft Teams calls in #91438 (see its proof + user-attachment evidence). This increment is verified by the full local gates on this branch: oxlint, `tsgo` prod types, `check:test-types`, and the voice-call suites — 130+ tests including new regression tests for the echo-guard playout clock, replay rejection, deterministic group gate, verbal interrupts, history-scope look, notify drain, slideshow caption, and per-source vision push. Live re-verification of the increment on a real Teams call is scheduled and will be added to this PR before it leaves draft.
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch:** `pnpm tsgo:prod && pnpm check:test-types && pnpm test extensions/voice-call` (all green). Live (pending, will update): place a Teams call on this build; confirm no self-answer + spoken barge-in still works; "stop" mid-reply cuts instantly; group call stays silent until addressed by name; "what did the earlier slide say?" answers from keyframes; `show_to_caller` renders a captioned image; an `openclaw_agent_task` "call me back" delivers and hangs up un-clipped.
- **Evidence after fix:**
  ```
  Test Files  4 passed (4)
       Tests  110+ passed  (voice-call: msteams provider / realtime / media-stream / config)
  tsgo: 0 errors · check:test-types: 0 errors · oxlint: 0 errors
  ```
  Foundation live evidence: #91438 (screenshots/video + redacted transcripts).
- **Observed result after fix:** All gates green at every commit; regression tests reproduce the original echo/replay/clipping failure modes and confirm the fixes (e.g. burst-generated audio: echo still suppressed at +2 s where the old last-send window had expired; exact handshake replay → 401).
- **What was not tested:** Live re-verification of this increment on a real Teams call (scheduled; unit/regression-tested only — foundation behavior was live-proven in #91438). Google realtime provider (`sendImage` is an optional method → safe no-op). Azure Speech visemes (this stack runs ElevenLabs + realtime → RMS + estimated visemes). Compliance-recording tenant policies beyond the recording-status gate.
